reviews.review_description:
​Hello, I’m really enjoying the game.
​In Eleven Table Tennis, there are graphic options that allow users to enhance the visuals and achieve smoother gameplay. Do you have any plans to implement similar graphic settings in Eleven Pickleball?
​I believe many players would love to have this feature, so I’m reaching out to inquire. Thank you!

This is an excellent simulation of real-life pickleball.  It is not a video game, so if you want a Wii-like game, this is not the game for you.  For context,  I am a 4.5 player and it is realistic and fun enough that I use this game almost every day to practice or warm up for real life games.   I spend about 30% of my time in the ball machine where I can create any shot imaginable (spin, angle, speed, variation), about 20% in various game created drills,  and about 50% playing bots.  (I'm probably an aberration though.  Most people will probably spend almost all of their time playing bots or other people in multiplayer games.)

I have had this game since it was in beta, and it has improved by leaps and bounds and continues to improve (more drills, more options for gameplay etc.)

reviews.review_title: It's veryrealistic to actual pickle ball
reviews.review_description: Online play is getting better as more and more subscribers to pickle ball arrive.  With online play its a hit and miss. You need to go on discord to play with more people.    You have to link it to discord to get friends to follow you so you can play more often. The physics and mechanics is as realistic to actual pickle ball you can get. Very happy with vr pickle ball and vr table tennis

developer_response.body:
Hi, 

Glad you like the physics

For MP, your review has the oposite effect, you are choosing to hurt the game instead of help it grow to have a large multiplayer.

We are going to manage the price of the game to get MP bigger.

Regardless a 2 star review only hurts the growth.

reviews.review_description: For playing pickleball it’s amazing very fun and engaging. I do have a small issue and it’s with the trainer I wish I could endlessly train, for those that don’t know you only get 10 tries on certain training modes and I wish you had the option for unlimited trained and mess ups but other than that small detail it’s amazing.
Dont mind what others say. Play this game and have fun. Even better if you have friends to play with. Hope they bring in new content soon.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic simulation, I have a pitch!
reviews.review_description:
​The game looks and feels wonderful. The graphics and performance put other games to shame. As a pickleball simulation, I think it does a great job, you really have to make sure you connect with the right part of the paddle (I imagine the adapter would help even more with accuracy).

​I'd love for the developers to make a new game/mode adapted specifically for typical VR spaces. This would be more of a workout and feel less immersion breaking than auto move. For a game like pickleball, I think this would mean a lower net height with a faster, smaller ball. Because the current mini court has the standard net height and ball, it doesn't quite achieve this (though it is great for practicing my short game). Keep up the great work!
Darn, its too bad that its now at a permanant discount w/ no compensation for early adopters. It is a fantastic version of Pickleball though.

I usually never review games but this one is truly one of the most amazing and realistic sports games I've ever played in VR. Works well in real scale but the automatic locomotion is also very useful when playing in small places. Highly Recommend!
If you like Eleven table tennis (one of the best games ever) you also like this game (the best pickleball game thus far imo). Great physics, great AI (especially fun with a human partner against AI).

I really like the dinking drills. It translates pretty well into my real life games. It’s more accurate than Pickleball One. 

I wish there were some more features for training, but understandably they can only invest time and money into development when the game gains more traction. 

One easy improvement they could do is provide an asset/skin for a two-tone ball so you can see how much spin you’re generating.

This app is amazing realistic. I have been able to use it to really hone my skills especially love the ball machine where I can work on shot mechanics. Spend the time to get to know the app get your padel setup properly and its as close to being on the court as you cab get. Excellent job highly recommend

reviews.review_title: Useless. Even with adaptor as a 4.5 play
reviews.review_description: Very disappointed. I was super excited to try. Please refund. Tried to set up an account. It bounced back. Is been wks. No response from developer.

developer_response.body: Hi, not true, we repsonded here immediately when you posted this review. Please reach out to elevenpickleball@forfunlabs.com to continue

reviews.review_description:
This game is fun and feels "like" a real game of pickleball. 
It does, however, have some physics issues.
Moving the paddle into a better position and changing movement settings is a must. But even afterwards I feel like I'm getting used to it and then a normal hit will fall short of the net. Then trying to compensate sends the next ball over the baseline.
It seems a little inconsistent.
I'm much better at the game in real life and find this vr version to undo skill development rather than be a fun training platform.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

I think you would have much better results with a paddle adapter. Many high level players in our discord verify they have improved as a result.

Simulating the weight and swing of real pickleball with a light controller that has no swing arm is not possible to do with perfect realism.

reviews.review_description:
I love the ability to be able to tweak a pickleball machine to my exact liking to drill shots.  Highly recommend getting an adapter as it adds a more life like feel.  

Physics and spin mechanics are great, excited to see it only get better from here.

This game is fun but isn’t nearly as good as their table tennis game. I’m an avid pickleball player in real life but I find the movement and paddle to be a little bit off but not enough to keep me from enjoying the game. I find the auto movement often puts me just out of arm reach of the ball. I also find that the ball doesn’t hit the paddle where I think it will so I make some rogue shots.  If I had rated this game when I bought it 2 years ago I would’ve given it 2 stars but you can see they are working to make the game better by fixing things and adding content. Hopefully after more updates in the future I can update it to 5 stars. I’m also going to give one of the paddle adapters a try and see if it fixes some of my issues.

developer_response.body:
Hi Shimeninja,

Thanks for the feedback, if you have very particular things in mind we can improve please do share them with me, precise feedback helps us move the product forward.

Thanks

reviews.review_description: Being a beginner, this game is fantastic to practice and enjoy competition. Love the realism. Been playing it daily 😀

I’ve waited over a year to write this review knowing it was early access and it wasnt fair to review an unfinished game. Sadly, every time I return after a couple months, the physics are continually worse… like WAY worse.  I barely tap the ball and it floats long out of bounds. If you swing like you would in real life, the ball will sail 100ft. It feels terrible. It kills me to leave a 1 star review for a dev who created a near perfect table tennis sim, but THIS game doesn’t live up to the high bar they previously set. Normally I’d hold out some hope that they’ll fix it but this game is going the wrong direction imho. 
Sorry for the harsh review… You’re still the undisputed king of Ping Pong though

developer_response.body:
This is far from the typical experience as you can read from many experienced pickleball players in our discord.

It simply sounds like you have a pretty glaring technical issue.

Instead of leaving a 1 star review you could simply reach out in our discord for technical support.

reviews.review_description: Came back and got this game on sale. Enjoying it more. Unsure what the mp would look like, couldn’t get a match
Its fun just wish there were more people on to play online
This game is super fun and I'm writing this for the free pickles

Fun way to ease into the game everyone's playing IRL!  I  appreciate being able to figure things out at home, not embarrassing myself out in public, or relying on the patience of serious players. 

I've talked a few friends into trying it with me and it is fun to play with them- having a bot demonstrate good form, mostly failing  to emulate them, but at least not having to chase balls all over creation.

I may be imagining things, but I think my hand-eye coordination is actually improving!

reviews.review_description: I have this (Eleven Pickleball) and Pickleball One, and this one I kept installed because it just feels less gamified for the physics and I want as realistic as possible. I have not played IRL yet but plan on starting in about a year, and have been using this to practice. I know some say the physics are not perfect yet and not playing IRL I cannot tell, but I do hope some of the skills translate to real life pickleball once I do start playing. I love the mini-court and all the training as I can do them without auto-movement (I'm not a big fan of auto-movement so the more options that allow a small space to practice with real movement the better). The slam practice staggers too much so is the only one I avoid, but I'm sure it will improve in time (perhaps longer time on ball resets). Also would be nice if we could free rotate the court (both grip triggers together maybe) rather than hold the button down in hopes that the sides align with the mat. Looking forward to see what is to come and the improvements made.
This game is smooth, realistic gameplay. I think I can improve my reflection when practice in this game.

I’m a 4.5-5.0 player irl and I have to say this game has a lot of potential but also has a long ways to go.. it’d be nice to see more accommodating to people with less space. Realistic scenarios too like kitchen games (kinda like pball one..). There’s mini and skinny’s atm but you have to move with controller around still unless you have a ton of space and mini is unrealistic. Just having a mode where you could play 1v1 kitchen games or 2v2 would be great. Also another easy fix would be ball sound. A more realistic sound would be a game changer. Physics has a ways to go too but isn’t bad. Spin is kinda weird and anything past mid court is very unrealistic. I have more suggestions but still I’m looking forward to the games future! Side note: Adapter is a must for realism, without it it’s not even worth
this game is very cool and i highly recommend to beginner vr players it's very easy to use and the teleportation feature is amazing
If you are a pickleball addict , then this is a must play game in vr
Ive been playing this game for hours and its actually helping me become a better player. It has great training modes, Ai is consistent. And game feels satisfying
The game is fun but the physics is kinda off and hard to get used to but still a fun game

reviews.review_title: Great Game could use some work
reviews.review_description: Great game, could use some movement work and would be pretty good. Paddle seems to sometimes be a bit off axis but that’s fine.

developer_response.body: Hi Juuddaahh, glad you like the game. The paddle is 100% repositionable however you like in the paddle settings menu. If you have any other feedback we welcome it, thanks for playing.

reviews.review_description: So fun and feels realistic

reviews.review_title: An easy way to play Pickleball
reviews.review_description: I believe the game is really helpful to practice Pickleball. I like how they build it to reduce spaces where you run automatically to the ball position and it feels good, especially combined with the mix reality option that allows you to have control of your space, and also has 3 different kinds of courts (big, medium, and small). Is very well integrated. It has 3 different ways of strength when hitting the ball. Still sometimes it looks like u send the ball very far or too short or the ball going left when you hit it to go right. Still room for improvement but it is fun and nice to play it even if it is not perfect yet. I'm sure the developers will continue working on this game because it is really amazing the work they just did and they will continue making it better and better. I totally recommend it.
This app is amazing. It feels real playing pickleball
Everyone should own this game

reviews.review_description: Certainly a work out , fun game , rules take some getting used to but I’m sure I’ll get better lol
Amazing game but needs a little work on ball physics and the ball hitting the paddle

I live in a small town in southern Italy with just one pickleball court (it's basically lines painted over a tennis court) and zero local pickleball culture or passion – which is a shame because I think the sport's an absolute blast. I rented the court with my VR headset and played for an hour against bots using free locomotion, aligning the mixed-reality court lines with the real ones: it was a blast! That said, I ran into two issues. First, after every single game, the court lines automatically shift and I have to recenter them. Getting it precise isn't easy and it eats up time. Second, in real life, I only play with folks who hardly know the rules, so I always win. Here? Even the Gold Bot (level 60) beat me – and this was my first try! But here's what really drives me nuts: the bot never moves up to the net after returning serve. I have no idea why it's programmed that way – it's just not realistic. Because the bot stays back, there's zero pressure on my third shot drop. I get all the time in the world to move up, and then it finally comes forward too. Worse, during net play, the bot never once tries to hit a passing shot. It just lives in the kitchen dinking or volleying – again, totally unrealistic. And get this: the bot only hits drives on the return of serve. After that? It's all dinks, all the time.  If they could fix these bot behaviors, this game would be a game-changer for practice. Even if you don't love the physics, it's an insane cardio workout and fantastic for sharpening your net reflexes. I use an adapter (though the controller is actually heavier than a real paddle), and I only notice two physics quirks: shots tend to float upward even when I swing flat and level, and I can't seem to put any spin on the ball. Still, I'd seriously recommend this experience on a real court to anyone – especially if, like me, you don't have real opponents to play with.

reviews.review_description: I’ve played 11 Pickleball for awhile now and it is worth the purchase price. The devs are constantly making improvements and are open to and often act on suggestions from the community. The only negative is the sometimes sparse multiplayer options, but I have confidence that 11 PB will become as popular as their table tennis counterpart as Pickleball is the fastest growing sport..

Physics are as excellent as in 11 table tennis. You do need to have a good 8 x 8’ area to play in because you will have to dive for a shots as they reach the edges of the court.(but you can limit the court to a mini court if your area space is only limited.) the tutorials were excellent at getting me started by learning how to serve, return various types of shots, and then play challenge games to understand how to control the ball and paddle relative to the controller.

Just as in it’s ping-pong based cousin, I generally don’t care very much about what the play environment looks like because I spend so much attention focusing on the ball play itself. This is a good thing and ideal for any sports oriented VR experience. ￼
Fun really fun
The movement is a little funky, but you will get the hang of it. Overall, super cool game. I love the different paddles ￼
Physics are really good
This game is very easy to play once you get the hang of it.  It's great to get that muscle memory back before you go live on a real court.  Very realistic.
It’s the best looking pickleball game, but it’s missing some features in Pickleball One, and I find the physics too frustrating.

Update: physics is a lot better for normal swings with the slime pickleball adapter. Adjusted my rating. 

(Old): Bought this when it was beta, and at that time felt unplayable. About a year later with updates, the game has improved a lot. Almost perfect,  except you cant really do fast ground stroke swings and serves as you would in real life. The physics isnt consistent.

developer_response.body:
Hi please read through this page please thank you, https://11pball.com/adapter

And please consider updating your review if it helps!

It has helped many who have had your criticisms, and ultimately solving this problem for users using only a light controller for a sport that has a paddle thats much heavier is just near intractable with current vr hardware unless unrealistic assists are put in place and we are not interested in going down that route, especially when the plastic adapter is top tier realism solution.

reviews.review_description: Good physics and fun game

The multiplayer is more or less dead which means you can only either drill or play against bots most of the time. The bots are more realistic than before, but now they frequently dink vertically with 0 power and spin and always just out of volley reach. That's been the case for a long time now with no fix promised from the dev either. The updates in this game come very slow too.

developer_response.body:
Incorrect. We've been consistently updating for over 2 years and super responsive in the discord, if you examine where the game has been going back in 6 months increments you would see major changes. To quote yourself "The bots are more realistic than before" indicating progress. Perhaps the updates have not been to your exact liking or preference, and on that we welcome feedback. That doesn't mean we will immediately implement anything you request.

The multiplayer base is going to be addressed in coming updates and promotions, which will cause a lot of influx.

Finally, leaving 1 star reviews, is really not the way to help a game you want to see grow.

Sorry you feel you have to behave poorly in our discord and come and leave this review because not all of your feedback (like many users requests) is implemented immediately.

reviews.review_description: It’s cool

Never played pickleball, but physics feel very realistic with great control (slice/spin, all worked for me). There are a nice variety of training modes and playing against AI allows you to choose many skill levels just like table tennis (the lower levels for beginners are not that easy to beat so nice challenge to ramp up skills).

There are multiple motion settings but including 'Auto Run' they require sometimes proactive user input adjustment to reach tougher balls. The 'Auto Run' now and then moves you the wrong location. UPDATE 6/'25: latest update fixes being too close to the ball at the net and remaining is sometimes that you won't move left/right with fast diagonal balls but this happens not very often. The override still doesn't truly stick, when I move back, it sometimes moves me right back again forward. I understand design to make it more realistic, but some auto teleport issues remain.

Couple of things to improve:
1) Pause is not working. If I want to adjust the motion settings it just keeps playing behind the settings dialog

2) In 'Auto Run' I found the adjustment override not intuitive: you can make general adjustment (1 of 4 quadrants basically), with pressing trigger it's fully manual. Nothing in-between. It would be nice to only override when you use trigger + movement (not just trigger), then when releasing it auto moves you towards the ball trajectory. Also would be nice if it would use the analog direction amount of the thumbstick to vary speed. Now it's fixed

3) I wish a regular typical (repurposed tennis) outside open court would be included beside the one in the clouds (but the clouds do look fantastic). There are few more courts but they all feel bit more confined in comparison

4) The announcer calls every ball out even if it isn't (e.g. double bounce) and says 'game over' after each game even if it isn't the last
This Pickleball is very fun and realistic, and has helped me improve my game￼
Great for small spaces with autorun feature.

This game is great and it entertained me for 
hours
